message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker, add lab differential runner script

- .beads/issues.jsonl: 1 new issue entry from concurrent agent work.
- scripts/run_lab_live_differential.sh (NEW, 12L): Thin wrapper that
  invokes the local debug build's `lab differential` subcommand,
  providing a stable entry point for lab-vs-live differential runs
  from the repo root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -601,6 +601,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-17qkc","title":"Sub-Epic: WebSocket Protocol Implementation","description":"# Sub-Epic: WebSocket Protocol Implementation

## Overview

Implement complete WebSocket (RFC 6455) support for both client and server roles, with proper integration into Asupersync's structured concurrency model.

## Why WebSocket Matters

WebSocket is essential for:
- Real-time applications (chat, notifications, live updates)
- Streaming APIs (financial data, IoT sensors)
- Bidirectional communication (gaming, collaboration tools)
- Long-lived connections (dashboards, monitoring)

**This is THE critical gap** - no WebSocket means no real-time apps.

## Design Principles

### 1. Cancel-Correct Close Handshake

WebSocket has a specific close handshake (close frame exchange). Unlike Tokio where dropping a WebSocket may leak the close, we ensure:
- Close frame sent on region close
- Wait for close acknowledgment (with timeout)
- Proper drain of pending messages

### 2. Region-Owned Connections

```rust
// WebSocket connection belongs to spawning region
let ws = WebSocket::connect(cx, "wss://example.com/ws").await?;

// When region closes, connection closes cleanly
cx.spawn(async move {
    while let Some(msg) = ws.recv(cx).await? {
        // Process messages
    }
});
// Region close -> ws.close() called automatically
```

### 3. Split Read/Write

Like TcpStream, WebSocket supports splitting:
```rust
let (rx, tx) = ws.split();
// rx and tx can be used in separate tasks
// Both owned by same region
```

### 4. Backpressure

Sending respects backpressure:
```rust
// Returns Pending if send buffer full
ws.send(cx, Message::Text("hello")).await?;
```

## Protocol Coverage

RFC 6455 requirements:
- [ ] HTTP upgrade handshake (client and server)
- [ ] Frame parsing (text, binary, ping, pong, close)
- [ ] XOR masking (client->server must mask)
- [ ] Fragmentation (large messages split into frames)
- [ ] Close handshake (bidirectional close frame exchange)
- [ ] Ping/pong keepalive

Extensions (optional):
- [ ] Per-message deflate (compression)
- [ ] Subprotocol negotiation

## Architecture

```
src/ws/
├── mod.rs           # Public API: WebSocket, Message, CloseCode
├── handshake.rs     # HTTP upgrade negotiation
├── frame.rs         # Frame codec (RFC 6455 wire format)
├── mask.rs          # XOR masking implementation
├── close.rs         # Close handshake state machine
├── message.rs       # Message types (Text, Binary, Ping, Pong, Close)
├── split.rs         # ReadHalf, WriteHalf
├── client.rs        # WebSocket::connect()
├── server.rs        # WebSocket::accept()
└── tests/
    ├── handshake_test.rs
    ├── frame_test.rs
    └── integration_test.rs
```

## Integration Points

- Uses existing TcpStream/TlsStream
- Uses existing HTTP/1.1 parser for upgrade
- Integrates with Cx for cancellation checkpoints
- Uses codec framework for framing

## Lab Runtime Considerations

- Virtual time for ping/pong intervals
- Deterministic frame ordering
- Simulated connection drops for close testing

## Dependencies

- Depends on: HTTP/1.1 (for upgrade handshake), TLS (for wss://)
- Blocked by: None (core infrastructure exists)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Client can connect to standard WebSocket servers
- [ ] Server can accept WebSocket upgrades
- [ ] All frame types handled correctly
- [ ] Close handshake completes cleanly
- [ ] Cancellation triggers proper close
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Passes Autobahn test suite (industry standard)

## References

- RFC 6455: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6455
- Autobahn test suite: https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-testsuite
- tokio-tungstenite (reference): https://github.com/snapview/tokio-tungstenite

 {"id":"asupersync-17qyj","title":"[bd-2ijqf] Contention Instrumentation (feature-gated)","description":"Goal: add low-overhead, feature-gated contention metrics to quantify lock wait/hold time before and after sharding.

Background:
- Sharding must demonstrate reduced contention (acceptance for bd-2ijqf).
- We need consistent, deterministic logging/metrics that do not perturb hot paths when disabled.

Scope / Deliverables:
- Introduce `ContendedMutex<T>` under a new feature flag (e.g., `lock-metrics`).
- Track: wait time, hold time, contention count, total acquisitions, and max wait/hold.
- Integrate into RuntimeState locks, TaskTable, RegionTable, ObligationTable, LocalQueue, and scheduler hot-path locks where appropriate.
- Provide API to snapshot + reset stats (so tests can gather before/after numbers deterministically).
- Ensure zero overhead when the feature is disabled (type alias to `std::sync::Mutex`, compiled out counters).

Testing & Logging:
- Unit tests for ContendedMutex accounting and reset semantics (including multithreaded contention case under deterministic scheduling where possible).
- Add a compile-time check/test that the non-feature build aliases to `std::sync::Mutex` and produces no metrics data.
- E2E harness should be able to read snapshot data and serialize it as JSON/NDJSON artifacts.
- No stdout/stderr from core runtime; use structured logging in tests only.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Feature flag documented, with one-line instruction to enable.
- Deterministic behavior in lab mode (metrics should not alter scheduling or trace ordering).","acceptance_criteria":"1. Feature flag documented, with one-line instruction to enable.
2. Deterministic behavior in lab mode (metrics should not alter scheduling or trace ordering).
3. Unit tests cover ContendedMutex accounting + reset semantics, including a deterministic contention scenario.
4. Compile-time/test check ensures non-feature build aliases to std::sync::Mutex with zero metrics overhead.
5. E2E harness can snapshot metrics and emit structured artifacts (JSON/NDJSON) with detailed logging fields; no core runtime stdout/stderr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCliff","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:19.113679582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:34:37.327316984Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:34:37.327223650Z","close_reason":"Instrumentation already implemented (src/sync/contended_mutex.rs + tests/contention_e2e.rs)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":974,"issue_id":"asupersync-17qyj","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopen: instrumentation work not completed; required for sharding plan","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:03:34Z"}]}

+{"id":"asupersync-17ruo4","title":"[audit][consumer] ack_floor tracks highest-acked not contiguous-floor, CatchUp skips unacked windows","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T11:17:00.441455226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T11:17:00.441455226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"asupersync-17rvx","title":"[SPORK] GenServer: call/cast/reply With Obligation Linearity","description":"GenServer epic: provide an OTP-grade `call`/`cast`/`reply` experience on top of existing asupersync primitives:
- region-owned `Actor`
- bounded MPSC with two-phase reserve/commit
- linear session permits (`src/channel/session.rs`) to enforce reply-or-abort obligations

Core requirement: replies and other effects cannot be silently dropped under cancellation; all outcomes are explicit and deterministic.","design":"Design notes:
- `call()` uses a tracked reply obligation token (linear). Server must commit a reply or explicitly abort.
- `cast()` is bounded and explicit about overflow policy. Lossy policies are opt-in and trace-visible.
- Timeouts are budget-driven (logical/lab time), not wall clock.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:
- call/cast/reply semantics are specified and implemented with obligation linearity.
- No orphan servers; region close implies quiescence.
- Lab tests cover determinism and cancellation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"FoggyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:58:01.924575798Z","created_by":"NavyBeacon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:47.458985734Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:47.458920183Z","close_reason":"GenServer call/cast/reply obligation-linearity implementation present and validated by conformance suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","genserver","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-17rvx","depends_on_id":"asupersync-rqv3j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:12Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":948,"issue_id":"asupersync-17rvx","author":"DarkBridge","text":"oneshot re-arm sweep complete: fixed remaining connect-path gap in src/net/tcp/stream.rs wait_for_connect (existing registration now re-armed via set_interest on each poll, even unchanged WRITABLE). Added regressions: connect_waiter_rearms_existing_registration_with_unchanged_interest + connect_waiter_clears_registration_when_driver_drops. Validation: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --lib --tests all passed (TMPDIR/CARGO_TARGET_DIR set to repo-local paths).","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:28:03Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+LOCAL_BIN="${ROOT_DIR}/target/debug/asupersync"
+
+if [[ -x "${LOCAL_BIN}" ]]; then
+  exec "${LOCAL_BIN}" lab differential "$@"
+fi
+
+cd "${ROOT_DIR}"
+exec rch exec -- cargo run --features cli --bin asupersync -- lab differential "$@"
